narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD weakness because DXY down 0.42% with USD scoring zero in CSM, reinforcing broad dollar selling across all majors
2. Risk-on momentum because S&P500 up 0.84% and copper up 1.98% are leading commodity-linked currency outperformance in NZD and AUD
3. CHF and NZD strength because CSM scores of 100 and 91 respectively confirm flow rotation into high-beta and safe-haven alternatives while USD is dumped

PRICE CONFIRMS

GBPUSD and EURUSD are moving in lockstep at 0.94 correlation confirming the dollar selling narrative is broad and not pair-specific. AUDUSD and NZDUSD are leading the risk-on leg with H4 buy signals at plus 3 and plus 4, consistent with copper and equity gains. CADJPY is lagging with a sell signal at minus 3, suggesting JPY crosses are diverging on CAD weakness rather than a systemic JPY reversal.

REGIME

Risk-On confirmed but with a caveat. S&P500 up 0.84% and copper up 1.98% anchor the regime, but VIX at 17.19 rising 0.64% and gold at 4720 also rising introduces mild hedging demand running beneath the surface, preventing a clean all-clear signal.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. VIX versus equities: VIX rising alongside S&P500 gains creates an unusual divergence suggesting some participants are buying protection into the rally
2. CHF strength versus risk-on: CHF scoring 100 on CSM while the regime is risk-on implies safe-haven demand coexists with risk appetite, contradicting a pure risk-on classification

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: GBPUSD up - strongest H4 buy signal, USD zero CSM, GBP riding dollar weakness cleanly - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: NZDUSD up - NZD CSM at 91, H4 buy confirmed, copper strength supports high-beta demand - Conviction 7/10
Avoid: CADJPY - CAD at 14 CSM creates noise, cross sends conflicting signals against JPY
Watch: DXY breaking and holding below 97.50 confirms primary GBPUSD trade and invalidates any dollar recovery thesis

themes.theme: Equity Market Strength and Labor Market Resilience
Commodity Complex Strength Across Grains and Energy

key_observation: Strong risk-on sentiment with equity strength, resilient labor market data, and broad commodity recovery supporting higher-beta currencies while pressuring the USD. Brazilian Real particularly strong on agricultural commodity tailwinds and currency-specific support.
watch: USD weakness acceleration if equity momentum continues; BRL sustainability above key levels; potential reversal if consumer sentiment data deteriorates further or geopolitical risks escalate beyond current priced-in levels
